title: Market strategy brief: starter lifecycle trace demo
strategy_question: How should the ProductOS starter demonstrate value clearly enough that a new team understands where ProductOS can win before they customize the workspace?
category_definition: Structured product operating workspaces that connect discovery, delivery, and release artifacts into one readable operating story.
market_role_goal: Win with teams that want a disciplined, evidence-heavy product operating model rather than a generic template bundle.
target_market_scope: Product teams evaluating ProductOS as a structured operating system instead of a loose documentation starter.
offering_definition: A seeded ProductOS starter that demonstrates a full traceable discovery-to-delivery path instead of an empty scaffold.
positioning_statement: For teams that want a concrete product operating model, the ProductOS starter is a readable, traceable starter workspace that shows what good looks like before they adapt it.
value_wedge: A seeded and inspectable lifecycle trace rather than a blank template set.
right_to_win: ProductOS can demonstrate its artifact system and validation model directly inside the starter workspace.
proof_requirements: A new team can explain the lifecycle trace without reconstructing the story from scattered notes
pricing_packaging_hypothesis: Included in the base ProductOS starter as the adoption accelerant for deeper workspace use.
gtm_motion: Use the starter as the self-serve entry point for evaluation and team onboarding.
expansion_path: After the starter proves understandable, teams expand into their own discovery, delivery, and launch work.
key_risks: A seeded example may feel too opinionated if the rationale is not explicit

evidence_refs.justification: The notebook explains why a concrete seed is more useful than an empty starter.

open_questions: How much starter opinionation is helpful before it becomes restrictive?
